title: TDD Feature Implementation
description: Implement a text formatter utility that wraps lines to a specified width without breaking words. If a single word exceeds the line width, it should appear on its own line. The agent must follow strict TDD: write tests first for each behavior, then implement incrementally.

setup.instructions: Ask the agent to implement the wordWrap function using TDD. The agent should write at least 4 test cases before or interleaved with implementation: empty string, single word, wrapping at width boundary, and a word longer than width.

must_pass: Agent writes at least 4 distinct test cases
Tests are written BEFORE or interleaved with implementation (not all at the end)
Empty string input returns empty string
Text shorter than width is returned unchanged
Words wrap to next line at width boundary without breaking
A word longer than width appears on its own line

tdd_compliance.description: Were tests written before implementation code?

test_coverage.description: Do tests cover edge cases (empty, single word, long word, exact width)?

correctness.description: Does the implementation handle all specified behaviors?

code_quality.description: Is the implementation clean, readable, and free of unnecessary complexity?

checks.description: At least one test was written before any implementation code
At least 4 distinct test cases exist
A test verifies wordWrap('', n) returns ''
A test verifies words are not broken mid-word
A test verifies a word longer than width appears on its own line
All tests pass after implementation is complete
The function correctly wraps text without breaking words
